# Search demo corpus

`corpus.jsonl` — 10,000 short synthetic "tech encyclopedia" documents, one
`{"title": ..., "body": ...}` JSON object per line (~2 MB). A document's id is
its zero-based line number.

**Provenance / license:** 100% synthetic. Every document is generated by
[`generate.mjs`](./generate.mjs) from the vocabulary in that script using a
fixed-seed PRNG, so the file is reproducible byte-for-byte and contains no
third-party data. Covered by this repository's MIT license.

**Regenerate:**

```bash
node generate.mjs
```

Both search engines consume the same bytes: the Rust crate embeds the file via
`include_str!` (so the compiled addon is self-contained), and the minisearch
baseline imports it through Vite's `?raw` — guaranteeing an identical corpus
on both sides of the A/B.
